title: The two-cell P0 projection preserves constant fields

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: Projecting a constant two-cell field onto constants returns the same constant on both cells.
assumptions: both projected coordinates are defined as the two-cell average
informal.expected_conclusion: The left and right projected cell values both equal u.

declarations.name: p0Projection2Left
declarations.statement: def p0Projection2Left (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := cellAverage2 u0 u1

declarations.name: p0Projection2Right
declarations.statement: def p0Projection2Right (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := cellAverage2 u0 u1

declarations.name: p0Projection2_preserves_const
declarations.statement: theorem p0Projection2_preserves_const (u : Real) : p0Projection2Left u u = u /\ p0Projection2Right u u = u

tasks.input: Translate constant preservation of the two-cell P0 projection into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem p0Projection2_preserves_const (u : Real) : p0Projection2Left u u = u /\ p0Projection2Right u u = u

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by splitting the conjunction and using the average-of-a-constant lemma on both coordinates.
tasks.target: Split the conjunction, unfold each projected coordinate, and use cellAverage2_const.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemma needed to prove each constant-projection coordinate.
